preview_text:
The model rates Scotland as the clear favorite at 45% to win, with Haiti at 24% and a draw at 30%. Scotland holds a 20-percentage-point edge over Haiti in outright victory probability, establishing them as the likely victors on a neutral surface.

The draw emerges as a competitive outcome in this matchup, sitting between the two outright results and representing roughly three in ten possible conclusions. The 20-point gap between the favorite and underdog indicates meaningful separation but leaves Haiti with a realistic path to upset or at minimum secure a share of the points. No single outcome dominates heavily; the match appears poised as a moderately open contest rather than one tilted sharply in either direction.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both diverge notably from the model, favoring Scotland substantially more. Books price Scotland at 62%, roughly 17 percentage points higher than the model's 45%. The market simultaneously compresses both Haiti and draw probabilities downward, suggesting materially greater confidence in a Scottish victory than the statistical forecast supports. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; material disagreement warrants caution in either direction.
